title: Pancreatic adenocarcinoma: smoking risk
vignette: A 65-year-old man comes to the office due to progressive weight loss, jaundice, and anorexia over the last 3 months. His urine has been dark and his stools have been pale. The patient has no prior medical conditions and takes no medications. He has smoked a pack of cigarettes a day for 40 years. He drinks 2 cups of coffee every day and 1 or 2 glasses of wine on most nights. He used illicit drugs for 2 years when he was in his teens but has used none since. The patient has a sedentary lifestyle and frequently consumes red and processed meats. Vital signs are within normal limits. BMI is 28 kg/m2. Physical examination shows scleral icterus. The chest is clear to auscultation and percussion. Abdominal examination shows an enlarged but nontender gallbladder. There is no ascites. Which of the following is the strongest factor predisposing to this patient's current condition?

clues.text: progressive weight loss, jaundice, and anorexia
clues.note: Painless progressive jaundice with weight loss and anorexia in an older adult is the classic presentation of pancreatic head adenocarcinoma compressing the common bile duct.

clues.text: His urine has been dark and his stools have been pale
clues.note: Dark urine + pale (acholic) stools indicate OBSTRUCTIVE (conjugated) hyperbilirubinemia — bile can't reach the gut. This points to biliary obstruction, e.g., a pancreatic head tumor.

clues.text: smoked a pack of cigarettes a day for 40 years
clues.note: Cigarette smoking is the single most important environmental/modifiable risk factor for pancreatic cancer, roughly doubling the risk. This is the answer the stem is after.

clues.text: 1 or 2 glasses of wine on most nights
clues.note: Bait toward 'alcohol.' Moderate alcohol without chronic pancreatitis is NOT an established pancreatic-cancer risk factor; alcohol's cancer links are liver, esophagus, and head/neck.

clues.text: frequently consumes red and processed meats
clues.note: Bait toward 'diet.' High red/processed-meat and low-fiber diets raise COLORECTAL (and gastric) cancer risk, not pancreatic. A decoy aimed at the wrong cancer.
A palpable, nontender, distended gallbladder in a jaundiced patient is the Courvoisier sign, suggesting malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head cancer) rather than gallstones.

whatMatters: This is a two-step item. Step 1: the painless obstructive jaundice (dark urine, pale stools, scleral icterus) + Courvoisier sign + weight loss = pancreatic head adenocarcinoma. Step 2 (the actual ask): of his many exposures, which is the STRONGEST risk factor? Cigarette smoking. Answer B. The wine/coffee/meat lines are decoys mapped to other cancers.
strategy: When a vignette lists many lifestyle exposures and asks for the STRONGEST predisposing factor, match each exposure to the cancer it actually causes: smoking -> pancreatic (and many others), alcohol -> liver/esophageal/head-neck, hot beverages -> esophageal, low-fiber/processed meat -> colorectal, viral hepatitis -> hepatocellular. For pancreatic cancer, smoking dominates.

eliminations.label: alcohol — links to liver/esophagus, not pancreas (your pick)
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: alcohol raises risk of head/neck, esophageal, and liver cancer and can cause chronic pancreatitis. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: moderate alcohol WITHOUT chronic pancreatitis is not an established pancreatic-cancer risk factor, and smoking is far stronger. The trap is equating nightly wine with pancreatic risk. ✅ Correct if: the tumor were hepatocellular, esophageal, or head/neck — or there were chronic alcoholic pancreatitis.

eliminations.label: coffee — no pancreatic risk
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: very hot beverages are linked to esophageal cancer. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: coffee does not increase pancreatic-cancer risk (0% chose it for good reason). ✅ Correct if: esophageal squamous cell cancer from chronic hot-beverage intake.

eliminations.label: low-fiber diet — colorectal, not pancreatic
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: low-fiber / high red-and-processed-meat diets raise COLORECTAL cancer risk. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: that's colon-cancer epidemiology, not pancreatic. ✅ Correct if: a colorectal adenocarcinoma question.

eliminations.label: processed meat — colorectal/gastric, not pancreatic
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: processed meats (nitrosamines) are associated with colorectal and gastric cancer. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: again colorectal/gastric — at most weakly pancreatic; smoking dominates. ✅ Correct if: colon or gastric adenocarcinoma.

eliminations.label: viral hepatitis — hepatocellular carcinoma
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: chronic HBV/HCV strongly cause HEPATOCELLULAR carcinoma. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: hepatitis raises pancreatic risk only slightly; this Courvoisier picture is pancreatic and the dominant risk is smoking. ✅ Correct if: cirrhosis with a liver mass and a rising AFP.

reasoning: Painless obstructive jaundice (dark urine, pale stools, scleral icterus) + weight loss + Courvoisier sign (enlarged nontender gallbladder) in an older man = pancreatic head adenocarcinoma
The question asks for the STRONGEST predisposing factor among his exposures
Cigarette smoking is the most important environmental risk factor for pancreatic cancer (roughly doubles risk)
mechanism: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma classically presents with painless obstructive jaundice (dark urine, pale stools, scleral icterus), weight loss, anorexia, and a Courvoisier sign (palpable, nontender, distended gallbladder) when a head tumor compresses the common bile duct. Among environmental exposures, cigarette smoking is the single most important risk factor, roughly doubling risk; other contributors include advanced age, chronic pancreatitis, obesity, and genetic predisposition (e.g., Peutz-Jeghers, hereditary pancreatitis, Lynch syndrome). The wine, coffee, and red-meat details are decoys that map to liver/esophageal/colorectal cancer, not the pancreas.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid GI/Onc > pancreatic adenocarcinoma: risk factors (smoking is the #1 environmental, plus age, chronic pancreatitis, genetics), Courvoisier sign, painless obstructive jaundice, migratory thrombophlebitis (Trousseau), CA 19-9.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: Two-step: (1) diagnose pancreatic head cancer from the jaundice + Courvoisier picture, then (2) pick the STRONGEST risk factor. Read past the decoy exposures to smoking.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on Courvoisier sign, obstructive vs hemolytic jaundice, scleral icterus, conjugated hyperbilirubinemia, Trousseau sign? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Alcohol (A) is the trap because he drinks nightly and alcohol 'feels' carcinogenic — but moderate alcohol isn't a pancreatic-cancer risk factor; smoking is strongest. Coffee/meat are decoys for esophageal/colon cancer.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank: pancreatic cancer's #1 environmental risk = smoking; don't be lured by alcohol/coffee/diet decoys aimed at other cancers.

keywords.front: Painless obstructive jaundice + Courvoisier sign + weight loss
Strongest environmental risk for pancreatic cancer
Enlarged nontender gallbladder in a jaundiced patient

firstAid.detail: GI/Onc > risk factors (smoking #1, age, chronic pancreatitis, genetics), painless jaundice, Courvoisier sign, CA 19-9, Trousseau.
firstAid.location: GI/Onc > risk factors (smoking #1, age, chronic pancreatitis, genetics), painless jaundice, Courvoisier sign, CA 19-9, Trousseau.

firstAid.detail: Onc > alcohol (liver/esophageal/head-neck), hot beverages/coffee (esophageal), low-fiber/processed meat (colorectal), viral hepatitis (HCC).
firstAid.location: Onc > alcohol (liver/esophageal/head-neck), hot beverages/coffee (esophageal), low-fiber/processed meat (colorectal), viral hepatitis (HCC).

firstAid.detail: GI > conjugated hyperbilirubinemia: dark urine, pale stools, pruritus; causes of biliary obstruction.
firstAid.location: GI > conjugated hyperbilirubinemia: dark urine, pale stools, pruritus; causes of biliary obstruction.

memoryHook: Pancreatic cancer's biggest risk is the SMOKE, not the wine. A big painless gallbladder (Courvoisier) -> think pancreatic head.
